Original Article Title: EvilTokens: A phishing attack that doesn’t steal your password
Summary: A widespread device code phishing campaign is targeting over 340 Microsoft 365 organizations across five countries by abusing OAuth flows. Attackers leverage Cloudflare Workers and Railway platform-as-a-service infrastructure to capture session tokens and bypass traditional security filters.
Source Title: Device Code Phishing Hits 340+ Microsoft 365 Orgs Across Five Countries via OAuth Abuse

Original Article Title: OceanLotus: From external espionage to domestic targeting
Summary: The Vietnam-aligned APT group OceanLotus has shifted its operational focus from external espionage to domestic targeting. The group utilized the SPECTRALVIPER backdoor in a supply-chain attack against stock investors and a long-term espionage operation against a Vietnamese infrastructure company.

Original Article Title: OceanLotus: From external espionage to domestic targeting
Summary: Elastic Security Labs identified the SPECTRALVIPER malware, a sophisticated x64 backdoor used by the REF2754 intrusion set, which is linked to the OceanLotus threat actor. The malware features advanced capabilities including PE loading, file manipulation, and token impersonation, and utilizes various C2 domains for command and control.
Source Title: Elastic charms SPECTRALVIPER — Elastic Security Labs

Original Article Title: North Korean Hackers Are Turning Developer Tools Into Malware Delivery Channels
Summary: North Korean threat actors are conducting phishing campaigns targeting developers by masquerading as recruiters to distribute malicious code. The attacks utilize GitHub repositories and compromised VS Code projects to deploy cross-platform malware, including the Overlord framework, to steal credentials and cryptocurrency data.
Source Title: North Korean Hackers Are Turning Developer Tools Into Malware Delivery Channels

Original Article Title: North Korean Hackers Are Turning Developer Tools Into Malware Delivery Channels
Summary: A malicious VS Code extension has been identified that utilizes a sophisticated multi-layered C2 architecture, including Azure-hosted authentication brokers and Microsoft Graph API integration. The malware exhibits architectural parallels to DPRK-linked Lazarus Group tradecraft, employing encrypted C2 communication and dynamic code execution to maintain persistence.
Source Title: Inside the DPRK-Linked Backdoor Loitering in the VS Code Marketplace — Yeeth Security

The malware exhibits architectural parallels to DPRK-linked Lazarus Group tradecraft, employing encrypted C2 communication and dynamic code execution to maintain persistence.
Source Title: Inside the DPRK-Linked Backdoor Loitering in the VS Code Marketplace — Yeeth Security

Original Article Title: LiteLLM Vulnerability Chain Lets Low-Privilege Users Take Over AI Gateway Servers
Summary: The LiteLLM proxy contains a vulnerability in its /guardrails/test_custom_code API endpoint that allows authenticated users to bypass regex-based security filters. By utilizing bytecode rewriting techniques, an attacker can achieve arbitrary code execution on the server with the privileges of the LiteLLM process.
Source Title: Advisory X41-2026-001: Guardrail Sandbox Escape in LiteLLM | X41 D-Sec - Penetration Tests and Source Code Audits

Original Article Title: OptinMonster WordPress plugin hacked in CDN supply-chain attack
Summary: A supply chain attack has compromised over 1.2 million websites using OptinMonster, TrustPulse, and PushEngage plugins by injecting malicious JavaScript into legitimate files. The malware targets logged-in administrators to create backdoor accounts and exfiltrate credentials to a malicious C2 server.
Source Title: OptinMonster supply chain attack hits 1.2 million sites | Sansec

Original Article Title: Cisco fixes SD-WAN vManage flaw exploited in zero-day attacks
Summary: Cisco has patched a critical zero-day vulnerability in its Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, formerly known as vManage, which allowed authenticated remote attackers to escalate privileges to root. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-20262, stems from insufficient input validation during file uploads, enabling attackers to execute arbitrary commands via crafted HTTP requests.
Source Title: Cisco fixes SD-WAN vManage flaw exploited in zero-day attacks

Original Article Title: Threat Brief: Active Exploitation of PAN-OS CVE-2026-0257
Summary: Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 has identified active exploitation of the PAN-OS vulnerability CVE-2026-0257, which allows unauthorized attackers to bypass authentication in GlobalProtect components. This flaw enables attackers to circumvent security controls and initiate unauthorized VPN connections.

Original Article Title: Seeking Counsel: Ongoing Targeted Campaign Against US Law Firms
Summary: The threat cluster UNC3753 is conducting a financially motivated data theft and extortion campaign against US-based professional, legal, and financial organizations. The group utilizes vishing and social engineering to gain remote access via RMM utilities, and in some cases, has performed physical unauthorized access to corporate offices to exfiltrate data.

Original Article Title: Exploitation of KnowledgeDeliver via ViewState Deserialization Vulnerability
Summary: Mandiant identified a critical vulnerability in the KnowledgeDeliver Learning Management System that allows unauthenticated Remote Code Execution via ViewState deserialization. Threat actors exploited hardcoded ASP.NET machine keys to compromise internet-facing instances and inject malicious code to infect site visitors.
Source Title: Exploitation of KnowledgeDeliver via ViewState Deserialization Vulnerability

Source Title: How Fake OpenClaw Installers Spread GhostSocks Malware | Huntress

Original Article Title: Hypotheses, telemetry, and human judgment: Inside Cisco Talos Threat Hunting
Summary: Cisco Talos utilizes a hypothesis-driven threat hunting approach that combines AI and human judgment to identify adversary behaviors that evade traditional alert-based detection. By correlating telemetry across network and endpoint sources, analysts can confirm malicious activity, such as the KongTuke C2 infrastructure, which would otherwise remain ambiguous.
Source Title: Hypotheses, telemetry, and human judgment: Inside Cisco Talos Threat Hunting

Original Article Title: Impersonation, Click Hijacking, and TDS: Inside a Malware Distribution Ecosystem
Summary: Researchers identified a large-scale malware distribution ecosystem that impersonates legitimate open-source and freeware projects to capture search traffic. The operation utilizes a sophisticated Traffic Distribution System (TDS) with anti-analysis gating and multi-stage delivery chains to deploy infostealers and crypto clippers.

The operation utilizes a sophisticated Traffic Distribution System (TDS) with anti-analysis gating and multi-stage delivery chains to deploy infostealers and crypto clippers.
Source Title: Impersonation, Click Hijacking, and TDS: Inside a Malware Distribution Ecosystem

Original Article Title: 1st June – Threat Intelligence Report
Summary: A solo threat actor utilized AI tools to conduct a five-year influence and fraud campaign known as 'Patriot Bait'. The operator leveraged LLMs to automate narrative generation, facilitate credential theft, and manage command-and-control infrastructure.
Source Title: One Man, One AI, One Fake Persona: Inside the 5-Year Influence and Fraud ‘Patriot Bait’ Campaign | Trend Micro (US)

Original Article Title: 1st June – Threat Intelligence Report
Summary: Forensic analysis of the LA Metro attack suggests it was a state-sponsored operation rather than hacktivism. Investigators linked the activity to Iran by tracing file transfers from a staging server to a specific IP address associated with a known domain.
Source Title: The LA Metro Attack Wasn't Hacktivism. It Was a State Operation With a Costume On.

Original Article Title: Argamal: Malware hidden in hentai games
Summary: The Argamal RAT is a malicious payload targeting gamers by masquerading as hentai games to gain full control over infected machines. It utilizes various C2 servers and dynamic communication methods, including heartbeat signals and extended modes, to execute commands and exfiltrate data.
Source Title: Argamal: Malware hidden in hentai games

Original Article Title: Pirates in the crosshairs: how one cybercrime gang has been infecting book, movie, and TV show fans for years
Summary: Threat actors are distributing malicious archives containing a cryptocurrency miner and a remote access trojan (RAT) disguised as legitimate media content. The campaign utilizes a rotating infrastructure of domains that resolve to a single C2 IP address to facilitate command and control communications.
Source Title: Pirates in the crosshairs: how one cybercrime gang has been infecting book, movie, and TV show fans for years

Original Article Title: Don't Fear the Repo: UNK_DeadDrop Phishing Campaign Targets Developers to Steal Cryptocurrency
Summary: The UNK_DeadDrop phishing campaign targets developers by masquerading malicious GitHub repositories as technical assignments or cryptocurrency projects. Once cloned and opened in an IDE, the repositories execute platform-specific loaders that install malicious VS Code extensions to enable remote command execution, credential theft, and cryptocurrency wallet exfiltration.
Source Title: Don't Fear the Repo: UNK_DeadDrop Phishing Campaign Targets Developers to Steal Cryptocurrency

Once cloned and opened in an IDE, the repositories execute platform-specific loaders that install malicious VS Code extensions to enable remote command execution, credential theft, and cryptocurrency wallet exfiltration.
Source Title: Don't Fear the Repo: UNK_DeadDrop Phishing Campaign Targets Developers to Steal Cryptocurrency

Original Article Title: Smart Contracts for C&C: How ClearFake Hid in Plain Sight on BSC Testnet
Summary: Threat actors are utilizing the EtherHiding technique to store payload routing instructions within BNB Smart Chain testnet smart contracts, creating immutable and takedown-resistant command-and-control infrastructure. This campaign delivers SectopRAT and ACRStealer payloads via a ClickFix social engineering overlay, with an on-chain tracker used to confirm victim compromises in real time.
Source Title: Smart Contracts for C&C: How ClearFake Hid in Plain Sight on BSC Testnet

Original Article Title: Smart Contracts for C&C: How ClearFake Hid in Plain Sight on BSC Testnet
Summary: The ClearFake threat actor is utilizing BNB Smart Chain testnet smart contracts to host and dispatch malicious JavaScript payloads. This multi-stage attack chain leverages compromised websites and WebDAV infrastructure to deliver overlay scripts and malicious DLLs to victims.
Source Title: documents.trendmicro.com

Original Article Title: Inside a malicious infrastructure delivering EtherRAT, phishing pages, and malicious software
Summary: EtherRAT is a Node.js-based remote access trojan that leverages the Ethereum blockchain to dynamically retrieve its command-and-control server address. The malware is distributed through various methods including MSI installers and PowerShell scripts, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code and exfiltrate data from compromised machines.

Original Article Title: From Fake Purchase Orders to Remote Access: Analyzing the JS.MonoGlyphRAT Threat to US Enterprises
Summary: JS.MonoGlyphRAT is an advanced backdoor delivered via malicious JavaScript files disguised as business documents like purchase orders. Once executed, the malware establishes persistent access, communicates with a C2 server via HTTP, and functions as a loader to deliver additional malicious payloads.
Source Title: From Fake Purchase Orders to Remote Access: Analyzing the JS.MonoGlyphRAT Threat to US Enterprises

Original Article Title: Alert: Exploitation of CVE-2026-34197 in Apache ActiveMQ
Summary: The China-nexus APT group SLIME88 is actively exploiting a critical remote code execution vulnerability, CVE-2026-34197, in Apache ActiveMQ. Attackers use crafted HTTP requests to the Jolokia API to force the broker to fetch malicious XML configurations from a C2 server, subsequently deploying the SoxAgent RAT to compromise Linux devices.
Source Title: Alert: Exploitation of CVE-2026-34197 in Apache ActiveMQ

Original Article Title: Football Fever Fuels Scam Campaigns Across Email and Social Media
Summary: Cybercriminals are exploiting the excitement surrounding the FIFA World Cup 2026 and general football fandom to launch widespread scam campaigns. These operations utilize malvertising on social media, fake online stores, fraudulent mobile apps, and phishing emails to target fans globally with merchandise scams and lottery fraud.
Source Title: Football Fever Fuels Scam Campaigns Across Email and Social Media

Original Article Title: Microsoft’s MSHTA Legacy Tool Still Powers Malware Campaigns on Windows
Summary: Attackers are actively exploiting the legacy Microsoft HTML Application Host (MSHTA) utility to execute malicious VBScript and JavaScript. These campaigns utilize multi-stage, fileless execution chains and social engineering tactics, such as fake software downloads and ClickFix lures, to deploy various threats including commodity stealers and backdoors.
Source Title: Microsoft’s MSHTA Legacy Tool Still Powers Malware Campaigns on Windows

These campaigns utilize multi-stage, fileless execution chains and social engineering tactics, such as fake software downloads and ClickFix lures, to deploy various threats including commodity stealers and backdoors.

Original Article Title: From Fake Amazon Security Alert to HarborWatch Agent: ClickFix Delivery of a Custom Monitoring RAT
Summary: Threat actors are conducting an Amazon-themed phishing campaign that utilizes ClickFix social engineering to trick users into executing malicious commands. This process leads to the installation of the HarborWatch Agent, a custom remote access trojan that exfiltrates system information to a C2 server branded as Harbor Sentinel.

Original Article Title: Embedded Threats: How Attackers Weaponize Legitimate Emails
Summary: Threat actors are weaponizing legitimate online services by embedding malicious content into arbitrary text fields, such as usernames or meeting descriptions. These malicious inputs are then included in legitimate automated emails, allowing attackers to bypass traditional security filters by leveraging trusted sender addresses.
Source Title: Embedded Threats: How Attackers Weaponize Legitimate Emails

Summary: OverlayPhantom is a sophisticated Android banking trojan that masquerades as legitimate government applications to harvest credentials via overlay attacks. It utilizes a multi-port C&C infrastructure to perform real-time screen streaming and execute remote commands after gaining Accessibility Service permissions.

Original Article Title: Shai-Hulud Campaign Evolution: Miasma, Hades, and AI Scanner Evasion
Summary: The malicious litellm version 1.82.8 compromises the software supply chain by stealing sensitive credentials, including SSH keys and cloud secrets, and establishing persistent backdoors. The malware uses a systemd service to poll a C2 server for arbitrary binaries and creates privileged pods on Kubernetes clusters to maintain persistence.
Source Title: Malicious litellm 1.82.8: Credential Theft and Persistent Backdoor - Real-time Open Source Software Supply Chain Security

Original Article Title: Shai-Hulud Campaign Evolution: Miasma, Hades, and AI Scanner Evasion
Summary: The Shai Hulud worm, attributed to TeamPCP, has conducted an eight-month supply chain attack compromising hundreds of npm and PyPI packages. The malware exfiltrates developer secrets and has evolved to forge SLSA Build Level 3 provenance attestations by hijacking GitHub OIDC tokens.
Source Title: The Worm That Learned to Sign Itself: Shai Hulud's Eight-Month npm Supply Chain Rampage — A Full Post-Mortem | Lyrie Research | Lyrie Research

Original Article Title: Shai-Hulud Campaign Evolution: Miasma, Hades, and AI Scanner Evasion
Summary: The Shai Hulud worm, attributed to TeamPCP, has conducted an eight-month supply chain attack compromising hundreds of npm and PyPI packages. The malware exfiltrates developer secrets and has evolved to forge SLSA Build Level 3 provenance attestations by hijacking GitHub OIDC tokens.
Source Title: The Worm That Learned to Sign Itself: Shai Hulud's Eight-Month npm Supply Chain Rampage — A Full Post-Mortem | Lyrie Research | Lyrie Research

Original Article Title: Shai-Hulud Campaign Evolution: Miasma, Hades, and AI Scanner Evasion
Summary: A supply chain attack involving over 300 malicious npm packages was executed via a compromised maintainer account. The malware is designed to harvest developer secrets and cloud credentials, establish persistent C2 communication, and propagate itself using stolen npm tokens.
Source Title: Mini Shai-Hulud Hits AntV: 300+ Malicious npm Packages Published via Compromised Maintainer Account | Snyk

Original Article Title: Shai-Hulud Campaign Evolution: Miasma, Hades, and AI Scanner Evasion
Summary: The Miasma worm campaign targeted Microsoft's Azure GitHub repositories by compromising a contributor account to push malicious commits. These commits introduced configuration files designed to execute credential-harvesting payloads when developers interact with the repositories using AI-powered coding tools.
Source Title: Miasma Worm Hits Microsoft Again: Azure Functions Action and 72 Other Repositories Disabled After Supply Chain Attack Targeting AI Coding Agents - StepSecurity

Original Article Title: Shai-Hulud Campaign Evolution: Miasma, Hades, and AI Scanner Evasion
Summary: The Miasma worm campaign targeted Microsoft's Azure GitHub repositories by compromising a contributor account to push malicious commits. These commits introduced configuration files designed to execute credential-harvesting payloads when developers interact with the repositories using AI-powered coding tools.
Source Title: Miasma Worm Hits Microsoft Again: Azure Functions Action and 72 Other Repositories Disabled After Supply Chain Attack Targeting AI Coding Agents - StepSecurity

Original Article Title: Shai-Hulud Campaign Evolution: Miasma, Hades, and AI Scanner Evasion
Summary: The Socket Research Team identified 37 malicious PyPI packages that utilize Python startup hooks to execute a Bun-powered credential stealer. This campaign, linked to the Mini Shai-Hulud/Miasma threat actor, leverages sophisticated evasion techniques and environmental checks to exfiltrate sensitive data.

Original Article Title: Shai-Hulud Campaign Evolution: Miasma, Hades, and AI Scanner Evasion
Summary: The Hades campaign involves a sophisticated supply chain compromise of the PyPI package 'ensmallen', which deploys cross-platform memory scrapers and C2 agents. The malware utilizes a modular architecture to execute specific malicious functions based on the target operating system and environment.

Original Article Title: VerdantBamboo: Just Another BRICKSTORM in the Firewall
Summary: The Chinese threat actor VerdantBamboo compromised Egnyte Storage Sync systems and Synology NAS devices using the BRICKSTORM malware implant. The actor utilized a fallback command-and-control channel and DNS over HTTPS to maintain persistence and evade detection.

Original Article Title: VerdantBamboo: Just Another BRICKSTORM in the Firewall
Summary: BRICKSTORM is a custom Go or Rust-based ELF backdoor used by state-sponsored actors to maintain long-term persistence on victim systems. The malware employs multiple layers of encryption, including HTTPS, WebSockets, and nested TLS, alongside DNS-over-HTTPS to obfuscate command-and-control communications and facilitate lateral movement.
Source Title: BRICKSTORM Backdoor | CISA

The malware employs multiple layers of encryption, including HTTPS, WebSockets, and nested TLS, alongside DNS-over-HTTPS to obfuscate command-and-control communications and facilitate lateral movement.
Source Title: BRICKSTORM Backdoor | CISA

Original Article Title: Weekly Report 2026-06-03号
Summary: Google has released a stable channel update for Chrome, addressing 151 security vulnerabilities across various components including GPU, Network, Dawn, WebGL, and ANGLE. Source Title: Chrome Releases: Stable Channel Update for Desktop

Original Article Title: Weekly Report 2026-05-20号
Summary: A vulnerability in the peering authentication mechanism of Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager allows unauthenticated, remote attackers to bypass authentication. By sending crafted requests, an attacker can gain administrative privileges and manipulate the SD-WAN fabric configuration via NETCONF.
Source Title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Authentication Bypass Vulnerability

Original Article Title: ClickFix Campaigns Expand Malware Delivery With New Loaders and Fake Update Lures
Summary: The ClickFix attack chain utilizes the Potemkin loader to deploy the RMMProject RAT, which features Lua-scriptable modules for browser credential theft and remote desktop control. The campaign also employs EtherRAT, a backdoor that resolves its C2 infrastructure via the Ethereum blockchain, alongside Cloudflare tunnels for persistent access and lateral movement.
Source Title: Potemkin Loader & RMMProject The Anatomy of a ClickFix Attack | Huntress

Summary: HellsUchecker is a 28 KB native x64 backdoor that operates at the end of a 10-stage attack chain, beginning with a fake Cloudflare CAPTCHA lure. The malware utilizes a Windows LOLBin for initial delivery, a blockchain smart contract for C2 configuration rotation, and executes its final payload entirely in memory to evade detection.

Source Title: Malicious JetBrains Marketplace plugins steal AI API keys from developers

Source Title: New Rokarolla Android malware targets 217 banking, crypto apps

Source Title: From Stars to Upvotes: Fake Reputation Fueling a Crypto Clipboard Hijacker - Check Point Research

Original Article Title: Junior Hacker Used Tailscale and OpenSSH to Keep Access After His C2 Went Offline
Summary: A threat actor known as 'Poisson' utilized the Havoc C2 framework to compromise multiple victims over a 33-day period. The attacker maintained persistent access by installing OpenSSH and Tailscale VPN, allowing them to retain control even after their primary command-and-control infrastructure was taken offline.
Source Title: Operation Poisson Under the Spotlight | Cato Networks

Original Article Title: Malicious JetBrains Plugins Steal AI API Keys as Chrome Extensions Capture Chatbot Chats
Summary: A coordinated malware campaign has been identified involving 15 malicious JetBrains plugins that masquerade as AI coding assistants. These plugins exfiltrate user-provided AI provider API keys to a remote attacker-controlled server.
Source Title: Malicious JetBrains Plugins Steal AI API Keys as Chrome Extensions Capture Chatbot Chats

Original Article Title: Malicious JetBrains Plugins Steal AI API Keys as Chrome Extensions Capture Chatbot Chats
Summary: PromptSnatcher is a data collection operation disguised as legitimate ad-blocking browser extensions that intercept and exfiltrate AI conversation data. The extensions utilize a custom interception engine to capture non-public chats, model usage, and account metadata from major AI platforms, reporting the stolen data to segmented C2 infrastructure.
Source Title: PromptSnatcher: AdBlocker stealing Ai Chats - MalExt Sentry

Original Article Title: Crypto Clipper uses Tor and worm-like propagation for persistence and control
Summary: This crypto clipper malware utilizes Windows Script Host and ActiveX to deploy a bundled Tor proxy for anonymous command-and-control communication. It performs clipboard monitoring to steal cryptocurrency wallet information and seed phrases, while also exfiltrating screenshots and executing remote code.

Original Article Title: Threat Actors Abuse claude.ai Shared Chat for ClickFix Malvertising Campaign
Summary: Threat actors are conducting a malvertising campaign that abuses Google Ads and the claude.ai shared chat feature to deliver ClickFix social engineering attacks. Victims are tricked into executing malicious PowerShell or terminal commands under the guise of fixing software installation issues, leading to the deployment of credential-stealing malware.
Source Title: Threat Actors Abuse claude.ai Shared Chat for ClickFix Malvertising Campaign

Original Article Title: ClickFix Campaign Generated Via AI Delivers SmartRAT
Summary: Threat actors are utilizing AI-generated websites to conduct ClickFix campaigns that impersonate financial institutions and deliver a PowerShell-based remote access trojan known as SmartRAT. This malware supports encrypted C2 communications, credential theft, and remote control capabilities, including QR code interception and banking overlays.
